channel.title: Blog on Tuan
channel.description: Recent content in Blog on Tuan

item.title: Deploy Angular App to Azure
item.description: This is a good guide that explains basic steps to deploy a Angular app to Azure. However, there are a few extra items that you might want to do to have your app run smoothly in production. Generate deployment.cmd Instead of using azure-cli, use kuduscript: npm install kuduscript -g and kuduscript -y --node Set up web.config <?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?> <configuration> <system.webServer> <rewrite> <rules> <clear /> <rule name="Redirect to https" stopProcessing="true"> <match url=".

item.title: Deploy Private Repo on Gitlab to Azure
item.description: Here are the steps to deploy your private repository on gitlab to Azure: Get Kudu’s public deployment key Kudu is the engine behind git deployments in Azure App Service. For Kudu to be able to read from a private git repository, we need to first get its public deployment key. Get the deployment trigger uri On Azure portal, go to your Web app’s Properties blade. It looks something like this

item.title: Install Openvpn Server on Azure
item.description: We’re going to install OpenVPN Server on a Ubuntu Server VM running on Azure. Steps: Go to portal.azure.com > Marketplace and search for Ubuntu Server. Select a Ubuntu Server image that we want and follow the steps on Azure Portal to create a new VM. I use a cheapest VM size that I can find in the list which is A0. It costs about $4/month. I want the VPN server to be on all the time, so I disable Auto Shutdown option.

item.title: Useful Chrome Extensions for Github
item.description: These are the two extensions I found that make browsing code on github a much better experience: Octotree Sourcegraph

item.title: Test
item.description: this is a test This is my first post. Tiếng Việt. Hôm nay là một ngày đẹp trời. Testing syntax highlighting function hello() { console.log('world'); }
